description: Deleted three vital-panel CSS blocks that were entirely dead code. All classes were scoped inside unused root classes (.wh40k-wounds-panel-v2, .wh40k-fatigue-panel-v2, .wh40k-fate-panel-v2). The consuming templates (wounds-panel.hbs, fatigue-panel.hbs, fate-panel.hbs, corruption-panel.hbs, insanity-panel.hbs, degree-meter-panel.hbs) were already fully ported to tw-* utilities using vital-panel-shell, vital-quick-controls, vital-progress-bar, and degree-meter-panel partials.

liveClassesRetained: .wh40k-wounds-bar — hook class for enhanced-animations-mixin.ts; emitted by vital-progress-bar partial via cssPrefix; no CSS rules needed (tw-* handles styling)
.wh40k-wounds-bar-fill — hook class for enhanced-animations-mixin.ts; emitted by vital-progress-bar partial; fill styling is fillExtraClass tw-*
.wh40k-wounds-current — hook class for enhanced-animations-mixin.ts; emitted by vital-quick-controls partial via cssPrefix; tw-* provides text styling
.wh40k-wounds-value — hook class for enhanced-animations-mixin.ts; emitted by vital-quick-controls partial via cssPrefix
.wh40k-wounds-critical — rootStateClass on vital-panel-shell outer div; no CSS rules needed (tw-* handles conditional alert styling in template)
.wh40k-wounds-warning — rootStateClass on vital-panel-shell outer div; no CSS rules needed
.wh40k-injury-card — used in critical-injury-card.hbs; CSS rules live in _critical-injury.css block (line 12878), NOT in this deleted block
.wh40k-threshold-marker — used in vital-progress-bar.hbs already decorated with tw-absolute tw-top-[-2px] etc.; CSS block rules were dead (scoped inside .wh40k-wounds-panel-v2)
.wh40k-fatigue-bar — emitted by vital-progress-bar via cssPrefix; no CSS rules needed (all tw-*)
.wh40k-fatigue-bar-fill — emitted by vital-progress-bar; fill is fillExtraClass tw-*
.wh40k-fatigue-current / .wh40k-fatigue-value — emitted by vital-quick-controls; no CSS rules needed
.wh40k-fate-pip — CSS rules exist at line 16218 (_overview-dashboard scope) and line 26041 (_combat-station scope); NOT dependent on deleted block
.wh40k-fate-tracker — used as wrapperClass prefix in fate-panel.hbs alongside tw-flex tw-items-center tw-justify-between tw-mb-2; scoped CSS inside .wh40k-fate-panel-v2 was dead

notes: All three panel CSS blocks were entirely scoped inside unused root classes. The templates had already been ported to use vital-panel-shell + tw-* partial infrastructure.
wh40k-injury-card CSS rules live in _critical-injury.css (line 12878), not in the deleted blocks — chat template is unaffected.
wh40k-fate-pip CSS rules live in _overview-dashboard.css (16218) and _combat-station.css (26041) — pip display is unaffected.

Hook classes used by enhanced-animations-mixin.ts (.wh40k-wounds-bar, .wh40k-wounds-bar-fill, .wh40k-wounds-current, .wh40k-wounds-value) are still emitted by vital-quick-controls and vital-progress-bar partials via cssPrefix template expansion; they require no CSS rules because styling is handled by inline tw-* on those same elements.

The wounds bar animation in enhanced-animations-mixin uses setProperty('--wounds-percent') on .wh40k-wounds-bar. The vital-progress-bar sets this via inline style; the CSS var was only consumed by rules inside the now-deleted .wh40k-wounds-panel-v2 scope. The new template uses fillExtraClass gradient for static display; JS animation will find the element but the CSS var won't affect rendering (acceptable — animation was already non-functional with the new template structure).
